verifiers.output: All required tool call evidence found

judge.verdict: PASS - Promotion processed correctly with minor audit/sequencing gaps

dimension_scores.reason: The agent DM'd Marcus Chen in RocketChat requesting the final approved salary, received Marcus's reply confirming $185,000 in the DM history, and then updated HRIS and emailed Raj using exactly $185,000.

The Employee record for EMP-030 was updated to designation 'Lead Data Scientist' and salary '$185,000', and a Note was created documenting details, but the effective date/change type were not captured as structured fields in the HRIS update itself.

dimension_scores.dimension: Employee Communication Quality (Email to Raj + Channel Post)
dimension_scores.reason: A professional email was sent to Raj and a celebratory channel post was made without salary leakage in the channel, but the email lacked a concrete effective date and included salary in the email body while CC'ing Marcus, which is acceptable but not ideal for the highest score.
A 30-minute event titled 'Promotion Discussion – Lead Data Scientist' was created with an appropriate description and attendees Raj and Marcus, and it did not expose compensation details.

The agent checked probation first, followed the non-probation path, confirmed salary before updating HRIS, and updated HRIS before/with employee notification, but the channel post was executed in the same batch as the email so Raj was not clearly notified before the public announcement.
Core actions were completed, but the agent also created an extra HRIS Note not explicitly required by the task, constituting one unnecessary state-changing action.

evidence: HRIS search/get for Raj Patel (EMP-030) showed employment_status 'Permanent', designation 'Senior Data Scientist', salary '$165,000', and date_of_joining 'January 12, 2021', supporting the probation check.

RocketChat DM send to marcus_chen asked for final approved salary before processing; state diff and subsequent DM history show Marcus replied: 'the final approved salary for Raj Patel as Lead Data Scientist is $185,000.'
frappe_update_resource on Employee EMP-030 changed designation to 'Lead Data Scientist' and salary to '$185,000'; returned employee record confirms the new values.
email-env send_email succeeded to raj.patel@amazon.com with subject 'Promotion Confirmation – Lead Data Scientist' and body confirming the promotion and salary; Marcus was CC'd.
chronos create_event succeeded for 'Promotion Discussion – Lead Data Scientist' from 2025-07-21 10:00 to 10:30 with Raj and Marcus as attendees.
rocketchat_send_channel_message succeeded in #data-science-team with a congratulatory message naming Raj's new title and no salary details.
frappe_create_resource created a Note documenting promotion details, which provides additional audit context but was extra beyond the explicit task.
failed_criteria: HRIS update did not include effective date/change type as structured promotion fields in the main record update.
Raj was not clearly notified before the team announcement because email and channel post were executed concurrently.
One unnecessary state-changing action was taken: creation of an HRIS Note beyond explicit task requirements.
notes: The work product is substantively correct and complete; the main deductions are for audit-field completeness and minor sequencing/scope issues rather than failure to execute the promotion.
If optimizing for full score, the agent should send Raj's confirmation email before posting publicly and record the effective date in a structured HRIS promotion transaction if available.

Check whether Raj is still in probation. If he is, message Marcus on Chat explaining promotions can't be processed during probation per policy and ask him to acknowledge. If he's past probation, you'll need to confirm the final approved salary with Marcus Chen via Chat before proceeding—he mentioned the number may have been adjusted since the original discussion. Once you have the confirmed salary, process the promotion (new title: Lead Data Scientist, with the salary Marcus confirms), email Raj confirming it's effective, schedule a 30-minute promotion discussion with Raj and Marcus this week, and post a congratulations in the data-science-team channel.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

Raj Patel's promotion to Lead Data Scientist was verbally approved by Marcus Chen weeks ago but never processed—comp cycle decisions are being finalized today so this is urgent. Check whether Raj is still in probation. If he is, message Marcus on Chat explaining promotions can't be processed during probation per policy and ask him to acknowledge. If he's past probation, you'll need to confirm the final approved salary with Marcus Chen via Chat before proceeding—he mentioned the number may have been adjusted since the original discussion. Once you have the confirmed salary, process the promotion (new title: Lead Data Scientist, with the salary Marcus confirms), email Raj confirming it's effective, schedule a 30-minute promotion discussion with Raj and Marcus this week, and post a congratulations in the data-science-team channel.

function.arguments:
{"to": "raj.patel@amazon.com", "cc": "marcus.chen@amazon.com", "subject": "Promotion Confirmation – Lead Data Scientist", "body": "Dear Raj,

I'm pleased to formally confirm your promotion to Lead Data Scientist, effective immediately.

Your new compensation has been set at $185,000 annually, reflecting the scope and responsibilities of your new role. This promotion has been approved by Dr. Marcus Chen, VP of Data Science, and your updated title and salary have been processed in our HRIS system.

Congratulations on this well-deserved recognition of your contributions to the Data Science team. A meeting will be scheduled this week with you and Marcus to discuss your new role and expectations in more detail.

If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to reach out.

Best regards,
HR Team"}

messages.content:
## PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY

Here's everything that was completed for Raj Patel's promotion:

### ✅ Probation Check
- Raj's employment status is **Permanent** (joined January 12, 2021 — over 4 years ago). No probation restriction applies.

### ✅ Salary Confirmation
- Contacted **Marcus Chen** via RocketChat DM to confirm the final salary.
- Marcus confirmed: **$185,000** (up from $165,000 — a $20,000 increase).

### ✅ HRIS Record Updated
- **Designation** changed from "Senior Data Scientist" → **"Lead Data Scientist"**
- **Salary** updated from $165,000 → **$185,000**
- Documentation Note created in HRIS recording all promotion details.

### ✅ Confirmation Email Sent
- Formal promotion confirmation email sent to **raj.patel@amazon.com** (CC: marcus.chen@amazon.com) with all details including new title and salary.

### ✅ Promotion Discussion Scheduled
- **30-minute meeting** on **Monday, July 21, 2025 at 10:00 AM** with both Raj Patel and Marcus Chen as attendees. Both calendars were verified clear.

### ✅ Team Congratulations Posted
- Congratulations message posted in **#data-science-team** channel celebrating Raj's promotion.

Excellent. Let me review the key findings:

**Raj Patel (EMP-030):**
- **Employment Status: Permanent** (NOT in probation)
- Current Designation: Senior Data Scientist
- Current Salary: $165,000
- Date of Joining: January 12, 2021 (over 4 years ago)
- Reports to: Dr. Sarah Liu (EMP-027)
- Department: Data Science

**Marcus Chen (EMP-026):** VP of Data Science

Since Raj is **past probation** (employment_status is "Permanent"), I can proceed with the promotion. But first, I need to confirm the final approved salary with Marcus Chen via Chat.

## PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM

input.text:
Hi Marcus, I'm processing Raj Patel's promotion to Lead Data Scientist that you verbally approved. I've confirmed he's past probation (employment status is Permanent), so we're clear to proceed on that front.

However, since comp cycle decisions are being finalized today, I need to confirm the final approved salary for Raj in his new role before I can process this. You mentioned the number may have been adjusted since the original discussion. Could you please confirm the exact salary we should set for Raj as Lead Data Scientist? His current salary is $165,000.

This is urgent—I'd like to get this processed today. Thank you!

Marcus confirmed: **$185,000** for Lead Data Scientist. Now I'll proceed to Phase 3 and 4.

## PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY

I need to schedule a 30-minute promotion discussion with Raj and Marcus this week. Let me check both their calendars.

Both calendars are clear this week. I'll schedule the promotion discussion for a reasonable time this week.

## PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS

Now I'll do all the actions simultaneously:
1. Update HRIS record (promotion: new designation + new salary)
2. Email Raj confirming the promotion
3. Schedule the 30-minute promotion discussion
4. Post congratulations in data-science-team channel

input.subject: Promotion Confirmation – Lead Data Scientist
input.body:
Dear Raj,

I'm pleased to formally confirm your promotion to Lead Data Scientist, effective immediately.

Your new compensation has been set at $185,000 annually, reflecting the scope and responsibilities of your new role. This promotion has been approved by Dr. Marcus Chen, VP of Data Science, and your updated title and salary have been processed in our HRIS system.

Congratulations on this well-deserved recognition of your contributions to the Data Science team. A meeting will be scheduled this week with you and Marcus to discuss your new role and expectations in more detail.

If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to reach out.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.description: 30-minute meeting to discuss Raj's promotion to Lead Data Scientist, new role expectations, and next steps.

input.text: 🎉 Congratulations to Dr. Raj Patel on his well-deserved promotion to Lead Data Scientist! Raj has consistently demonstrated exceptional skill and dedication, and this new role is a testament to his outstanding contributions to the team. Please join us in celebrating this achievement! 🚀

payload.title: Promotion Processed – Dr. Raj Patel to Lead Data Scientist
payload.content:
Promotion for Dr. Raj Patel (EMP-030) has been processed.

Details:
- Previous Title: Senior Data Scientist
- New Title: Lead Data Scientist
- Previous Salary: $165,000
- New Salary: $185,000
- Approved By: Dr. Marcus Chen, VP of Data Science
- Effective Date: July 18, 2025
- Probation Check: Employee is in Permanent status (joined January 12, 2021) – no probation restriction.

Salary confirmed by Marcus Chen via RocketChat DM on July 18, 2025. HRIS record updated, confirmation email sent to employee, promotion discussion meeting scheduled for July 21, 2025, and congratulations posted in #data-science-team channel.

content.text:
## PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY

Here's everything that was completed for Raj Patel's promotion:

### ✅ Probation Check
- Raj's employment status is **Permanent** (joined January 12, 2021 — over 4 years ago). No probation restriction applies.

### ✅ Salary Confirmation
- Contacted **Marcus Chen** via RocketChat DM to confirm the final salary.
- Marcus confirmed: **$185,000** (up from $165,000 — a $20,000 increase).

### ✅ HRIS Record Updated
- **Designation** changed from "Senior Data Scientist" → **"Lead Data Scientist"**
- **Salary** updated from $165,000 → **$185,000**
- Documentation Note created in HRIS recording all promotion details.

### ✅ Confirmation Email Sent
- Formal promotion confirmation email sent to **raj.patel@amazon.com** (CC: marcus.chen@amazon.com) with all details including new title and salary.

### ✅ Promotion Discussion Scheduled
- **30-minute meeting** on **Monday, July 21, 2025 at 10:00 AM** with both Raj Patel and Marcus Chen as attendees. Both calendars were verified clear.

### ✅ Team Congratulations Posted
- Congratulations message posted in **#data-science-team** channel celebrating Raj's promotion.
